summary: Small-scale paired register from Incahuasi (UE 22, separate from peanut area UE 13-18). 362 cords, 9 colors, perfectly paired (L2/L1=1.02). However, 53% of cords are L-1 (UNREADABLE — NULL turn data) and 41% are empty, making this essentially ILLEGIBLE for ALBA purposes. 0 readable STRING. Total value 1,353 = smallest of the five Sector A khipus (0.2% of UR268's 556,242). Structure and color analysis only — no text, no narrative possible.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  362 cords (L1: 173, L2: 176, L3: 13). Architecture: 1-PAIRE (L2/L1 = 1.02 — perfectly paired).
  9 colors: W(195), AB(65), MB(40), GG(19), NB(16), YB(14), KB(11), PG(1).
  26 clusters. Excavation: Sector A, Subsector 1, UA 4, Unidad 20N, UE 22.
  Different UE from peanut area (UE 13-18 = UR270/278/277/268).
  0 readable STRING, 191 INT, 148 EMPTY (41%), 191 L-1 (53% UNREADABLE).

DATA QUALITY WARNING
  The most DATA-DAMAGED khipu analyzed:
    191/362 cords (53%) have NULL turn data (L-1)
    148/362 cords (41%) are empty
    Only ~33 cords (~9%) have both non-zero values AND readable knot types
    0 STRING — all potential text cords are L-1
  Interpretation based on STRUCTURE and COLOR only.

STRUCTURE
  L2/L1 = 1.02 (perfectly paired). Total L1+L2 = 1,353.
  MB cords carry largest values (mean=20.5). YB all non-zero (10/10) = active category.
  Small values throughout: mean ~6, max 54.

THE FIVE KHIPUS OF SECTOR A
  UE 13: UR270 (total 9,209) — peanut warehouse
  UE 16: UR278 (total 59,166) — astronomical observatory
  UE 17: UR277 (total 5,533) — short-term inventory
  UE 18: UR268 (total 556,242) — peanut district summary
  UE 22: UR272 (total 1,353) — small-scale paired register (SMALLEST)
  UR272 at UE 22 = different storage purpose from the peanut area.

WHAT COULD BE DONE
  Physical re-examination with modern techniques could reveal up to 23 STRING words from L-1 cords. Fiber analysis, dye analysis, and radiocarbon dating would add context.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Structural analysis, data damage assessment
Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: The Inkawasi Khipu Archive
Cross-corpus: UR270, UR277, UR278, UR268 — the four other Sector A khipus
